column1: # AWS ElastiCache node-type catalogue — capacity source for synthkit ElastiCache construct.
#   memoryGiB = USABLE cache memory (AWS "Memory" column = maxmemory for Redis; engine overhead
#   already removed). This is NOT total instance RAM. E.g. cache.r6g.large = 13.07 GiB usable
#   on 16 GiB total RAM. Values taken from the "Supported node types" table on that page.
# burstableBaselinePct: per-vCPU baseline CPU % for t-family burstable nodes.
#   Same credit schedule as EC2 t3/t4g. Source:
# Note: m4/r4 families included as they remain orderable in eu-west-1 (legacy; prefer m5+/r5+).
#       c7gn (network-optimized Graviton3) enumerated but excluded — not a typical cache node type
#       for standard Redis workloads. r6gd (NVMe-SSD) enumerated but same CPU/RAM as r6g.
# --- General Purpose: cache.m4 (Intel Xeon E5-2666 v3; legacy) ---
# --- Memory Optimized: cache.r4 (Intel Xeon E5-2686 v4; legacy) ---
# --- Memory Optimized: cache.r6gd (NVMe-SSD; same CPU/RAM as r6g) ---
# --- Network Optimized: cache.c7gn (Graviton3; high-bandwidth; listed for completeness) ---
